name: Scottish Shortbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Place butter in a mixer and beat with a paddle until soft and light.
3. Beat in sugar in a stream, continuing to beat for 5 to 10 minutes until the mixture is very light and whitened.
4. Stir in flour by hand until it is fully absorbed; do not overmix or the dough will become tough.
5. Scrape the dough onto a floured surface and gently knead with your hands.
6. Roll the dough out once or twice very gently with a rolling pin until it is about 1/2-inch thick.
7. Cut the dough into desired shapes using floured cutters and transfer to a parchment-lined baking sheet.
8. Bake for approximately 15 minutes, or until lightly colored. Allow to cool on a wire rack.
9. For molded shortbread, press the mold into the floured dough and cut around it. Transfer the shaped dough to a parchment-lined baking sheet and chill for about an hour before baking as directed.
